headline: Kelantan Lost 14,000 Hectares of Forest in 2024
context: Satellite data from Global Forest Watch confirmed Kelantan lost 14,000 hectares of primary forest between January and December 2024, the highest in Peninsular Malaysia.

cards.big: Kelantan says logging is 'sustainable' and within annual limits.
cards.sub: Satellite imagery shows 14,000 hectares lost in 2024 — triple the state's own reported figure of 4,800.

cards.big: Only 38% of Kelantan's original forest cover remains.
cards.sub: The Forestry Department's 2024 annual report omits satellite-verified clearings in Gua Musang district.

cards.big: 12 logging concessions overlap with Orang Asli customary land.
cards.sub: None of the 12 concessions underwent Free, Prior and Informed Consent as required by UNDRIP.

cards.big: Timber royalties contributed RM280M to Kelantan's 2024 budget.
cards.sub: That is 22% of state revenue — creating a structural dependency on continued deforestation.

cards.big: When the state profits from the trees it is supposed to protect, who enforces the rules?
Federal oversight of state forestry is constitutionally limited but not impossible. Article 76(1)(b) allows Parliament to legislate for uniformity.
